channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Gun jams as woman tries to shoot boyfriend: Tennessee police
item.description: A Tennessee woman is facing an attempted murder charge after reportedly attempting to shoot her boyfriend before her gun jammed, Memphis Police said.
item.dc:creator: Raven Moore, ABC 4

item.title: Walmart recalls 12,000 cartons of chicken broth
item.description: Walmart has recalled over 12,000 cartons of Great Value Family Size Chicken Broth due to packaging issues that could lead to spoilage, according to the Food and Drug Administration (FDA).
item.dc:creator: Reagan Wynn, KREX 5

item.title: Winter weather road tips: Staying safe while navigating the snow and ice
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — Snowfall brings a whole bundle of headaches for drivers, but there are some steps that can be taken to avoid lingering issues or a potentially dangerous situation.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden honors Pope Francis with the Presidential Medal of Freedom
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Saturday honored Pope Francis with the Presidential Medal of Freedom with distinction, the highest civilian award given by the president, saying the pontiff was “a light of faith, hope, and love that shines brightly across the world.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Turkey's Kurdish leaders meet jailed politician as the two sides inch toward peace
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — A delegation from one of Turkey's biggest pro-Kurdish political parties met a leading figure of the Kurdish movement in prison Saturday, the latest step in a tentative process to end the country’s 40-year conflict, the party said.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW WILKS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Sam Moore, who sang ‘Soul Man’ in Sam & Dave duo, dies at 89 due to surgery complications
item.description: Sam Moore, the surviving half and higher voice of the 1960s duo Sam & Dave that was known for such definitive hits of the era as “Soul Man” and “Hold On, I'm Comin,’” has died. He was 89.
item.dc:creator: KRON4

item.title: FEMA kicks hurricane survivors out of temporary housing into snowstorm and freezing temperatures
item.description: Approximately 2,000 North Carolina households that survived Hurricane Helene will have to move out of their FEMA-expensed hotel rooms on Saturday amid a winter storm.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Californians scrambled to evacuate dogs, cats, horses, even pigs as LA flames closed in
item.description: Animal owners in Los Angeles have scrambled to get themselves and their beloved companions out of the way of fast-moving wildfires this week that have incinerated whole neighborhoods.
item.dc:creator: Hallie Golden And Leslie Ambriz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Powerful winter storm that dumped snow in US South maintains its icy grip
item.description: Snow and ice across the U.S. South are expected to relinquish their grip only slowly as the weekend continues.
item.dc:creator: Kate Brumback And Travis Loller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: LA wildfires destroy numerous houses of worship. Clergy and congregants vow to persevere
item.description: Flames were already attacking the campus of Pasadena Jewish Temple and Center when the cantor, Ruth Berman Harris, and three companions rushed in to rescue its sacred Torah scrolls.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, LUIS ANDRES HENAO and PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Powerful winter storm dumps heavy snow, causing flight delays, slick roads and school closings
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A powerful winter storm that dumped heavy snow and glazed roads with ice across much of Texas and Oklahoma lumbered eastward into Southern U.S. states Friday, making for dicey travel and a rare snow day for many students.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK and TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What ignited the deadly California wildfires? Investigators consider an array of possibilities
item.description: Investigators are considering an array of possible ignition sources for the huge fires that have killed at least 11 people and destroyed thousands of homes and businesses in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: JASON DEAREN, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: LIVE: Fires devastating LA grow more slowly as winds die down
item.description: The two biggest fires devastating the Los Angeles area grew just slightly Friday as firefighters beat back the blazes that have killed at least 10 people, obliterated neighborhoods and left the nation's second-largest city on edge.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KRON4

item.title: Brooklyn real estate magnate pleads guilty to conspiring to funnel illegal donations to NYC mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Brooklyn real estate magnate pleaded guilty Friday to working with a Turkish government official to funnel illegal campaign contributions to New York City Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rare gold coin found in red kettle donations for The Salvation Army
item.description: A rare South African Krugerrand gold coin worth thousands was found amid the spare change donated to a Salvation Army red kettle in Colorado.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Orleans terrorist attack bodycam shows Bourbon Street chaos as gunfire rings out
item.description: New Orleans Police Department body camera footage shows officers responding to the scene of a New Year's terrorist attack on Bourbon Street that left 15 dead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Polymarket allowing users to bet on deadly LA wildfires
item.description: An American prediction market platform is allowing users to place bets on deadly wildfires in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Photos: Smoke, smoldering homes and flames as wildfires ravage Los Angeles area
item.description: All around Los Angeles, scenes of flames were everywhere, as many areas continued to burn all but unabated.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How to donate to LA wildfire relief
item.description: A swarm of winter wildfires ravaged America's most heavily-populated county to start 2025, scorching cars, banks, bungalows and multi-million dollar mansions.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to uphold law that would force TikTok to shut down on Jan. 19
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday seemed likely to uphold a law that would ban TikTok in the United States beginning Jan. 19 unless the popular social media program is sold by its China-based parent company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Distraught LA mom confronts Newsom over flailing wildfire response
item.description: A desperate Los Angeles mother confronted California Gov. Gavin Newsom on Thursday about the city's raging wildfires, demanding answers about why fire hydrants ran empty and what he was going to do to fix communities that have been reduced to ash.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: From Carter's funeral, an American snapshot: A singular image, 5 presidents, 379 years of history
item.description: Look at their faces — formal, etched with experience, laden with the weight of momentous decisions. Add up their years: 379 to be exact, enough time to take you back into the mid-1600s, when the notion of the American nation was still more than a century away.
item.dc:creator: TED ANTHONY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: LIVE: Fires devastating LA grow more slowly as winds die down
item.description: The two biggest fires devastating the Los Angeles area grew just slightly Friday as firefighters beat back the blazes that have killed at least 10 people, obliterated neighborhoods and left the nation's second-largest city on edge.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about thousands of evacuations and homes burned in Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — At least 10 people were killed and thousands of structures were burned as fierce wildfires raged in the Los Angeles area, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Las Vegas Raider Charles Snowden thought he was in Florida during DUI arrest: Video
item.description: Tests showed Snowden’s blood-alcohol level was more than double the legal limit after officers found him asleep and unresponsive in a running SUV, documents show.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: Kroger agrees to pay $110M to Kentucky in opioid settlement
item.description: Coleman filed the lawsuit against Kroger in February 2024, alleging the grocery chain had failed to monitor for suspicious opioid orders.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation

item.title: Aerial video shows before and after views of Palisades Fire in Los Angeles
item.description: “I couldn’t image what we were preparing to see once we got over here on this side of the hill,” pilot Rich Prickett said.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: Jean Smart calls on TV networks to not air award shows, instead donate to Los Angeles wildfire relief
item.description: Jean Smart is urging TV networks to refrain from airing glitzy awards shows amid the devastating wildfires in the Los Angeles area, and to donate money to relief efforts instead.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Lawsuit by New Orleans truck attack victims says city, contractors failed to implement safety system
item.description: Six people who were injured and the father of a man who was killed in the New Year's truck attack filed a lawsuit Thursday against the City of New Orleans and two contractors, claiming they failed to protect revelers from an Army veteran who sped around a police blockade and raced down Bourbon Stree
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires leave hundreds of thousands without power
item.description: An outage map provided by the Los Angeles Department of Water and Power (LADWP) showed just how widespread the outages extended
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, NewsNation

item.title: Firefighters battle devastating Los Angeles wildfires as winds calm somewhat
item.description: EDITOR’S NOTE: Click HERE to view The Associated Press’ wildfire tracker, which shows where wildfires are spreading.
item.dc:creator: MANUEL VALDES, HALLIE GOLDEN and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Eaton Fire victim dies holding a hose, defending home
item.description: One of the five confirmed fatalities from the destructive Eaton Fire was identified on Wednesday morning when his family found his body on the side of the road by their home – with a garden hose still in his hand.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KRON4

item.title: Wildfires burning in the Los Angeles area visible from space
item.description: Satellite images from NOAA and CIRA show the Palisades and Eaton Fires burning in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, ABC 4

item.title: New book alleges that someone at Fox News gave Trump's team early look at town hall questions
item.description: Someone inside Fox News gave Donald Trump's presidential campaign the questions he would face ahead of a town hall the network produced in January 2024, a forthcoming book alleges, according to excerpts reported by CNN.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter's life intersected with slavery's legacy. His record on Civil Rights is complicated
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Young Jimmy Carter and his friends were walking across a pasture after a day’s farm labor during the Great Depression. As they came to a gate, his companions stood aside and let Carter enter first. This was no act of kindness or intuitive deference to a future U.S. president.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Harris's California neighborhood ordered to evacuate as wildfires blaze through area
item.description: Vice President Harris's neighborhood in Los Angeles was ordered to evacuate Tuesday night as wildfires blazed through the area, her spokesperson said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: 4 fraternity members at San Diego State University charged after one was set on fire during a 'skit': DA
item.description: The incident occurred last February, when the fraternity was on probation with the university over issues of hazing and conduct that threatens the health and safety of students.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, WGN 9
